calendar.matters:
Petition For (1)Determination That The Amendment To The Madge Marie Ulman 2004 Revocable Trust Is Void And To Impose A Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Aug-23-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Greg Alsanian appeared by zoom for Judy Jones (Petitioner).
Kelly Dankbar appeared by zoom for Karen Aman (beneficiary), Kathy Martinez (beneficiary) and Sirus Heick (executor of David Heick).
Edward Thirkell appeared by zoom for Linda Bickel (beneficiary).
Anthony Matricciani appeared by zoom for Wells Fargo Bank.

Mr. Aslanian stated that he filed a declaration regarding service, that discovery is being conducted and that some discovery has been received. The court heard oral argument from all parties with respect to their positions regarding the issue of whether or not proper venue is in San Francisco versus Texas. Upon conclusion of argument, the Petition for Determination that the Amendment to the Trust is Void and to Impose Constructive Trust is dismissed for lack of venue.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For (1)Determination That The Amendment To The Madge Marie Ulman 2004 Revocable Trust Is Void And To Impose A Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Jul-21-2021 Continued To Aug-23-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For (1)Determination That The Amendment To The Madge Marie Ulman 2004 Revocable Trust Is Void And To Impose A Constructive Trust
Off Calendar May-26-2021 Continued To Jul-21-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Greg Aslanian, via ZOOM, counsel for Judy Jones, Petitioner; KellY Dankbar, via ZOOM, counsel for Caryn Aman, Cathie Martinez, and Cyrus Heick, Objectors; Andre Zabronsky, counsel for Wells Fargo, Objector.

The court begins by querying counsel as to why venue is proper in San Francisco and why it appears that the Trust is being administrated from both San Francisco and Texas. Mr. Aslanian reports that the decedent lived and died in San Francisco and cites case law to support his argument regarding what is considered proper venue and jurisdiction. Additional argument is presented by counsel including Ms. Dankbar's request that the court dismiss the petition. The court then turns to Mr. Aslanian to point to the several procedural issues noted by the Examiner which include, but are not limited to, improper notice to Kaye Marshall, the several unidentified Does and Wells Fargo having been improperly named as a Respondent. Mr. Aslanian adds that efforts have been made to locate Faye Hill but those efforts have proved ineffective. He also reports that he has had issues working on this matter due to several issues caused by the pandemic.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Determination, etc., is continued to July 21, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
